title: Robotic Molecular Synthesis Lab
description: A robot chemist stands at a virtual lab bench surrounded by floating ShaderBall atoms representing chemical elements. The user must guide the robot to select specific atoms, drag them into a reaction chamber, adjust temperature and pressure parameters, and trigger synthesis to form target molecules while validating stoichiometric ratios.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap/CDN) implementing a robotic molecular synthesis lab. Load robot.glb as a lab assistant positioned at origin facing forward, and load ShaderBall.glb six times as floating atoms arranged in a semicircle around the robot at radius 4, each at varying heights (y: 1-3). Assign each ShaderBall a distinct emissive color and label (H=white, O=red, C=gray, N=blue, S=yellow, Cl=green) using sprite text labels above each. Camera: perspective at (0,4,8) looking at origin with OrbitControls. Lighting: ambient 0.4 intensity plus two directional lights simulating lab fluorescents. Implement these interaction steps: (1) A UI panel shows a target molecule formula (e.g. H2O, CO2, NH3, H2SO4) randomly selected on load. (2) User clicks atoms to select them—selected atoms glow brighter and orbit inward toward the robot. (3) A counter tracks selected atom counts per element. (4) User must match exact stoichiometry; a 'Validate' button checks if selected atoms match the formula, showing red error text if wrong. (5) User adjusts a temperature slider (200-2000K) and pressure slider (1-100 atm) to within valid ranges displayed as hints. (6) A 'Synthesize' button triggers the robot to play its animation (if available, else bob up/down), selected ShaderBalls lerp together to a central point above the robot forming a cluster, particles emit from the merge point, and a success banner appears. (7) A 'Reset' button clears selections and picks a new target molecule. Validate that temperature is within ±200K of target and pressure within ±10 atm of target. Use raycasting for atom selection. Add a subtle grid floor with metallic material. All UI uses HTML overlay elements styled as a sci-fi lab console with dark background and cyan accents.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  selectedHydrogen: number,         // count of selected hydrogen atoms
  selectedOxygen: number,         // count of selected oxygen atoms
  selectedCarbon: number,         // count of selected carbon atoms
  selectedNitrogen: number,         // count of selected nitrogen atoms
  selectedSulfur: number,         // count of selected sulfur atoms
  selectedChlorine: number,         // count of selected chlorine atoms
  temperature: number,         // current temperature in Kelvin (200-2000)
  pressure: number,         // current pressure in atmospheres (1-100)
  synthesisComplete: boolean,         // whether synthesis has been successfully completed
  validationAttempted: boolean,         // whether user has attempted validation
  parametersValid: boolean,         // whether temperature and pressure are in valid range
  targetMolecule: string,         // formula of the target molecule to synthesize
}
